metadata.title: SYNGO_MF_1982
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.1: congenital myasthenic syndrome patient has a E59K mutation in the nAChR delta subunit.
Fig.2+3+4: The mutation was shown to affect channel kinetics with heterologous expression of WT and δE59K mutant receptors in HEK cells.

Note: data to support the action of this mutation in situ is not included in this paper, but the annotation to the term 'transmitter-gated ion channel activity involved in regulation of postsynaptic membrane potential (GO:1904315)' is based on the presence the myasthenic syndrome in the mutation carrier and the well established localization of the nAChR in the postsynaptic membrane of muscle fibers.. Experimental description: Data is on heterologously expressed receptors of human species; "Because the patient phenotype suggested an effect of the mutations in utero as well as on mature muscle, we investigated functional effects of the δ subunit mutations on both fetal- and adult-type human AChR. cDNAs encoding mutant δ subunits in combination with wild-type α, β, ε, or γ were transfected into HEK293 cells, and cell surface 125I-α-BuTx was measured"
